category_title: Black eighth-grade students engaging in performances of resistance during writing revision
minimal_context: The student is reviewing the first draft of an essay with a tutor in a special education classroom to prepare a revised version for her general education class.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: You have completed your first draft. Now we need to work on revising it so you have a piece of writing to take back to your general education class.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: I have some suggestions for how you might revise it.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Why do I have to write it all again?

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: It is necessary to complete the task of drafting and revision.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Rolls her eyes and raises her voice) I already finished! (Walks out of the classroom)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: You have completed your first draft. Now we need to work on revising it so you have a piece of writing to take back to your general education class.

STUDENT: I already finished.

TUTOR: I have some suggestions for how you might revise it.

STUDENT: Why do I have to write it all again?

TUTOR: It is necessary to complete the task of drafting and revision.

STUDENT: (Rolls her eyes and raises her voice) I already finished! (Walks out of the classroom)

task.content: Revising a first draft of an essay for an English class
task.learning_goal: completing the task of drafting and revision so she would have a piece of writing to take back to her general education class

context.environment: special education classroom in New York City

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: when I made suggestions for how she might revise it
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: she became outraged, stating that she had already finished and asking why she had to write it all again. She rolled her eyes, raised her voice, and walked out of the classroom.
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: perceived my suggestions as unfair criticism... felt vulnerable and angry

behavior_patterns.condition: when I made suggestions for how she might revise it
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: became outraged, stating that she had already finished and asking why she had to write it all again. She rolled her eyes, raised her voice, and walked out of the classroom.
behavior_patterns.internal_state: perceived my suggestions as unfair criticism
